topic: ACL main page 10 Ethical Considerations linebreak and disclosure polish

defect.class: body text linebreak and closing-section prose polish
before_observation: Page 10 Ethical Considerations shared the page with References and showed body-line splits such as unsafe de-ployment, fil-tering, responsibil-ity, and visual pan-els.
Naive shortening moved the References heading to the bottom of the left column, so the accepted fix had to preserve the right-column References start.
defect.reader_effect: The final reviewer-facing disclosure block was readable but visually choppy and slightly less ACL-like than the surrounding closure sections.

changes.summary: Rewrote the risk/disclosure sentences with shorter claim-boundary language while preserving the synthetic-asset, license, safety, AI-tool disclosure, and evidence-scope boundaries.
Localized high hyphen penalties and sloppy paragraph setting to Ethical Considerations only, avoiding body end-of-line hyphenation without changing the rest of the paper.
Added an explicit disclosure-scope sentence: tool-use disclosure does not widen the empirical claim scope.

after_evidence.targeted_scan_result: Pages 9-10 show no accepted body-column end-of-line split words in Ethical Considerations; remaining hyphen matches are reference-column hyphenation or the literal in-line term manuscript-support.
after_evidence.visual_result: Figure 4 remains stable, Ethical Considerations fills the left column, and References starts cleanly at the top of the right column.

claim_boundary: Disclosure and linebreak polish only. No empirical result, table value, figure evidence, citation, or supported/forbidden claim scope was changed.
residual_risk: References retain normal bibliography hyphenation in narrow columns; this round intentionally treated only the Ethical Considerations body as the blocking reader-facing defect.
